evidence_summary: MeSH lists mechanical ventilation as an entry term under artificial respiration.
MeSH maps Artificial Feeding to Nutritional Support.
Life support sustains/prolongs life; artificial heart replaces pumping function.
Renal dialysis/kidney artificial evidence sits in replacement therapy and life-prolonging care contexts.
notes: Needs historical snippet if chart uses explicit line.

evidence_summary: MeSH defines artificial limbs as prosthetic replacements.
Artificial womb is closer to extracorporeal gestational support than conventional organ replacement.
notes: Mark as weak/edge; avoid causal line.

evidence_summary: Both intervene in reproductive process; artificial insemination facilitates fertilization.
notes: Needs older textual history if emphasized.

evidence_summary: MeSH IVF definition covers direct handling/manipulation to achieve fertilization in vitro.
notes: Good transition from older phrase to modern technique.

evidence_summary: Artificial womb is biological support/gestation; artificial life is computational life-process simulation.
Britannica notes A-life overlaps with adaptive systems and neural networks/AI questions.
Artificial language predates AI and provides a human symbolic-process anchor.
notes: Useful to keep language visible before AI.

evidence_summary: Britannica places neural networks in AI and cognitive computing.
FCC ruling links artificial voice language to AI-generated human voices and voice cloning.
Voice/presence can support companion systems, but current evidence is more topical than historical.
Artificial consciousness source stresses discipline remains theoretically obstructed.
Britannica frames AGI as controversial and out of reach.
